name: Housing Opportunities Of Fort Worth
description_short: HUD-approved nonprofit housing counselor in Fort Worth offering free homebuyer education, default counseling, and reverse mortgage guidance to low-to-moderate income families.
description_long:
Housing Opportunities of Fort Worth (HOFW) is the oldest HUD-approved housing counseling agency in Tarrant County, established in 1988 as a private 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization. The organization was founded with a specific mission to help low-to-moderate income residents of Tarrant and contiguous counties access and maintain affordable homeownership through structured education and personalized counseling. Over its 30+ year history, HOFW has built partnerships with major local organizations including Habitat for Humanity, the City of Fort Worth, the Fort Worth Housing Authority, and AARP. In 2018 alone, the agency's counselors served over 500 households.

HOFW provides three primary services: homebuyer education programs, default and delinquency counseling for struggling homeowners, and reverse equity mortgage counseling for seniors. All services are delivered on an individual basis at no cost to clients. The agency maintains bilingual capacity, offering all counseling and education in both English and Spanish. The organization has received funding from HUD, major foundations, and the mortgage lending industry, ensuring sustained operations and credibility.

What distinguishes HOFW is the depth of experience among its small staff. The four-person team collectively brings over 67 years of housing counseling expertise. Executive Director David O'Brien Jr. has 30 years of experience; Director of Counseling Cara Smith has 21 years; Senior Counselor Maria Santamaria has 16 years; and Office Manager Elena Luna has 15 years of administrative experience. Two staff members are bilingual Spanish speakers. This level of consolidated expertise is uncommon in nonprofit housing counseling.

HOFW operates as a traditional nonprofit with limited marketing presence and a straightforward service model. The organization's website is minimal and the service area is geographically limited to Tarrant and contiguous counties. As a free service funded by grants and industry support, HOFW has no financial incentive misalignment with clients. However, prospective clients should note that services are delivered individually rather than through group classes, which may result in longer wait times during peak demand periods.
pros: HUD-approved housing counselor with 30+ year track record and oldest such agency in Tarrant County
All services completely free with no hidden fees or financial products to sell
Bilingual staff offering counseling in English and Spanish
Specialized expertise in reverse mortgage counseling for seniors, an underserved population
Combined 67+ years of counseling experience among staff members
Successfully served 500+ households in 2018, demonstrating scale and trust
Partnerships with major local organizations including Habitat for Humanity and City of Fort Worth
cons: Limited geographic service area restricted to Tarrant and contiguous counties only
Minimal web presence and no online scheduling system based on website content
Individual counseling model may result in longer wait times compared to group education sessions
No information about appointment availability or typical wait times on website
Small staff of four people may limit capacity during peak demand periods
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Tarrant County seeking free education and personalized guidance
Homeowners facing default, delinquency, or foreclosure needing counseling and workout options
Seniors exploring reverse mortgage options and needing objective financial counseling
diagnosis: HOFW is ideal for low-to-moderate income residents of Tarrant County seeking free, unbiased guidance on homeownership or reverse mortgages from experienced, HUD-certified counselors. The primary limitation is geographic service area—this organization only serves Tarrant and contiguous counties, and residents outside this region should seek alternative HUD-approved agencies.
services: Bilingual counseling in Spanish and English
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 1065 W Magnolia Ave Ste A, Fort Worth, TX 76104
